channel.title: Software options on RangeShifter
channel.description: Recent content in Software options on RangeShifter

item.title: RangeShifter 3.0
item.description: The RangeShifter platform was first published in Bocedi et al. (2014) and new functionality was added in Bocedi et al. (2021) and in the latest 3.0 release. The software is coded in C++ and packaged as a freely available batch interface and for the previous 2.0 version also as Windows GUI. The graphical user interface is particularly useful for initial model exploration and for demonstration and teaching purposes. However, it is currently not maintained and only available for the RangeShifter v2.0 release. If you want to use the most recent release or run more complex or computationally demanding simulations, please use the batch interface.

item.title: RangeShifter GUI 2.0
item.description: The RangeShifter platform was first published in Bocedi et al. (2014) and new functionality was added in Bocedi et al. (2021). The software is coded in C++ and packaged as a freely available as Windows GUI. The graphical user interface is particularly useful for initial model exploration and for demonstration and teaching purposes. However, it is currently not maintained and only available for the RangeShifter v2.0 release. If you want to use the most recent release or run more complex or computationally demanding simulations, please use the batch interface.

item.title: RangeShiftR
item.description: We here introduce the RangeShiftR package 3.0, based on the first version developed by Malchow et al. (2021). The R package makes it especially easy to run more complex simulations, it allows more coherent workflows and easier reproducability as well as integration with other R packages and plotting functions.
